interaction.prompt: 5-on-3 PP, no shots in 30 seconds. PK is collapsing tight to the slot. Move the puck — what's the right read?

feedback.right: Cross-ice to LW for the one-timer. The PK over-loaded strong side; the LW has clean ice for a release.
feedback.wrong: Force-shooting from the bumper hits sticks. Move the kill — cross-ice opens the weak-side flank's release.

tip: 5-on-3 fails when shots are forced. Move the puck side-to-side, make the PK ROTATE, then release on a clean look.
why: PKs collapse on shooters. Cross-ice passes that beat a rotation are the highest-percentage 5-on-3 shot — the goalie is out of position before the puck arrives.
